---
title: "The End of Mobile Software Engineering (As We Know It)"
author: "Prof. Dr. Robin Nunkesser"
format: revealjs
---

# Mobile Software Engineering

## Before 2008

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

![](../../images/talks/BlackBerry-Curve-8320-313.png)

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

**Complex installation**

![](../../images/talks/black/operamini.png)

<span style='font-size: xx-small;'>Source: <a href="http://www.operamini.com">http://www.operamini.com</a></span>

:::
::::

## 2008

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

![](../../images/talks/App_Store_10th_anniversary_iPhone_first_gen_07102018_inline.jpg.large_2x.png)

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

**Introduction of the App Store**

![](../../images/mobile/one-billion-apps.png)

:::
::::

## Exponential Growth

![](../../images/talks/black/appstore.svg)

<span style='font-size: xx-small;'>Source: <a href="https://www.pocketgamer.biz/metrics/app-store/app-count/">https://www.pocketgamer.biz/metrics/app-store/app-count/</a></span>


## Recognized Research Need

> Despite the development of 300,000+ mobile applications, there’s still not much formal research around their engineering processes.

Wasserman (2010)

---

> [...] the growth of this new computing platform has outpaced the software engineering work tailored to mobile application development.

Dehlinger and Dixon (2011)

## Research Agenda of Wasserman (2010)

- User Experience 
- Code reuse
- Networking
- Energy efficiency
- Data integrity
- Testing
- Portability and cross-platform development

## Dedicated Conferences

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

![](../../images/talks/black/DeMobile2013.svg)

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

![](../../images/talks/black/MobileSoft2014.svg)

:::
::::

# The End of Mobile Software Engineering (As We Know It)...

## MOBILESoft vs ICSE

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

![](../../images/talks/black/mobilesoftpubs.svg) 

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

![](../../images/talks/black/icsemobilepubs.svg)

:::
::::

## Research Agenda

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

![](../../images/talks/black/wassermancits.svg)

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

![](../../images/talks/black/sekeys_all.svg) 

:::
::::

# ...And I Feel Fine 

## Mobile Is Eating The World

![](../../images/talks/black/oshistory.svg) 

## End of Need for Separate Research

- Mobile is more relevant than ever
- Separate research need is coming to an end
- Good opportunities for future research 

## Existing Research

- Reconsider research results
- Is mobile-specific research generalizable?
- Are there undiscovered _classic_ results applicable to mobile software engineering?

## Future Research

- Mobile-related research should consider generalizability
- Software Engineering research should consider implications for mobile devices 

# Thank you for your attention!
